mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving near-term equity outperformance over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable consumer price inflation signals a healthy demand-driven expansion without overheating, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL changes remain under 0.2% month-over-month, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, decelerating real GDP growth alongside sustained employment gains indicates peak corporate profitability, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of late-cycle episodes despite decelerating GDPC1 and rising PAYEMS, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side capacity utilization, providing a direct positive catalyst for equity valuations over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO month-over-month changes against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity multiples do not compress and 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5, or the signal reverses direction during quantitative tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in the unemployment rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor market absorption, which fuels consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when UNRATE and PAYEMS move in the specified directions, or the joint signal fails to outperform single-proxy baselines in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, surging retail sales combined with rebounding housing starts indicate rapid consumer confidence restoration, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 fails to produce positive 5-day returns in over 50% of post-shock episodes where RSAFS and HOUST both show month-over-month growth, or the signal loses predictive power when adjusted for FEDFUNDS changes.
